message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py, Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding backup/backup_suffix params to Image.save() to auto-backup existing files before overwrite

type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
summary: Adding dry_run parameter to Image.save() to process without writing to disk

type: INTENT
files: src/PIL/Image.py:2469-2591 (save method)
summary: Adding dry_run param: pop from params early, use io.BytesIO() as fp when dry_run=True to skip real file open/create/close, no other structural changes to save()

type: STATUS
files: src/PIL/Image.py:2469-2601 (save method), Tests/test_image.py (~line 754)
summary: Done: added backup/backup_suffix kwargs to save() signature (keyword-only, after format, before **params), added shutil import at top, and backup copy logic (shutil.copy2) inserted right before the 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' line inside 'if open_fp:' block. Added 4 new tests after test_no_new_file_on_error. Should be additive/non-conflicting with your dry_run param as long as you pop dry_run before my backup logic runs and don't restructure the open_fp block ordering drastically - please check line numbers shifted by my edits before you save.

type: ANSWER
files: src/PIL/Image.py:2469-2591
summary: Ack - my dry_run pop happens right after save_all pop, before encoderinfo merge, and I only changed the created/fp-open branch to add 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO()' as a new leading branch; didn't touch your backup insertion point ordering. Each of us submits patch.txt independently so no shared-file clobbering risk on my end.
